title: Application crashes on startup when DATABASE_URL is missing
body:
## Bug Report

The app exits with an unhandled exception instead of a clear error message when DATABASE_URL is not set.

**Steps to reproduce:**
1. Remove DATABASE_URL from the environment
2. Start the server

**Expected:** helpful error message and clean exit
**Actual:** `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'connect')` with a full stack trace dumped to stderr

**Impact:** New developers can't onboard without debugging this. Happens every time someone clones the repo without copying .env.example.

triage.summary: App crashes with an unhelpful stack trace when DATABASE_URL is missing instead of printing a clear error and exiting cleanly.
triage.suggestedAction: Add an early env-var guard in the startup sequence that checks for DATABASE_URL and exits with a descriptive message before attempting to connect.

scout.explanation: DATABASE_URL is passed directly to the Database constructor on line 44 with no null-check. When the env var is absent, the constructor throws a TypeError and the process exits with an unhandled exception and a cryptic stack trace.

title: SQL injection possible in /api/users/search endpoint
body:
## Security Issue

The `q` query parameter in `/api/users/search` is interpolated directly into a raw SQL string without sanitization.

```
GET /api/users/search?q='; DROP TABLE users; --
```

This executes successfully on the staging environment. Confirmed with a read-only payload; did not attempt destructive queries.

**Affected file:** `src/routes/users.ts` line 84

Suggest switching to parameterized queries or an ORM method.

triage.summary: The /api/users/search endpoint interpolates user input directly into a raw SQL string, confirmed exploitable on staging.
triage.suggestedAction: Replace the raw string interpolation in src/routes/users.ts:84 with parameterized queries or an ORM binding method.

scout.snippet: const rows = await db.query(`SELECT * FROM users WHERE name LIKE '%${q}%'`);
scout.explanation: The query parameter q is interpolated directly into the SQL string on line 84 without sanitization or parameterization, making it trivially exploitable via SQL injection.

title: Dashboard takes 8+ seconds to load with >500 records
body:
## Performance

The main dashboard query does a full table scan. With our current dataset (≈2,000 rows) the page takes over 8 seconds on production.

Adding an index on `(user_id, created_at)` on the `events` table brought it down to 120ms in local testing.

Migration script attached. No schema changes, index-only.

triage.summary: Dashboard performs a full table scan on the events table causing 8+ second load times in production with ~2,000 rows.
triage.suggestedAction: Apply the proposed migration to add a composite index on (user_id, created_at) to the events table.

title: Password-change emails not sent when user updates via profile page
body:
## Bug

When a user changes their password through `/settings/profile`, the confirmation email is never sent. The email fires correctly when the password is reset via the forgot-password flow.

Looked at the code briefly — it seems `sendPasswordChangedEmail` is called in the reset handler but not in the profile update handler (`src/controllers/profile.ts`).

triage.summary: Password-change confirmation emails are sent in the forgot-password flow but omitted in the profile-update flow.
triage.suggestedAction: Call sendPasswordChangedEmail in the profile update handler in src/controllers/profile.ts, mirroring the reset handler.

title: Pagination controls invisible on dark mode
body:
## UI Bug

The pagination arrows on the users list page use a hardcoded `color: #333` which disappears against the dark background. Affects users who have enabled dark mode in their OS.

**Screenshot:** (attached)

Quick fix: replace the hardcoded value with the `--color-text` CSS variable already used elsewhere.

triage.summary: Pagination arrows use a hardcoded color:#333 that becomes invisible on dark backgrounds.
triage.suggestedAction: Replace the hardcoded color value with the --color-text CSS variable already used elsewhere in the stylesheet.

title: Update README: API rate-limit section is out of date
body: The README still documents the old rate limit of 100 req/min. Since the v2 rollout it's 500 req/min for authenticated clients. Causing confusion in the community Discord.

triage.summary: The README documents the old rate limit of 100 req/min; the actual limit since v2 is 500 req/min for authenticated clients.
triage.suggestedAction: Update the rate-limit section in the README to reflect the current 500 req/min limit for authenticated clients.
